ddt:Target.Name: C1AHS_Evo_Side_Radar_Rear_Left_v1.3.0
ddt:Target.Description: V1.0.0 - 23/02/2019 : Creation base - Non officiel V1.1.0 09.03.2022 - BSIS screen, DTCs added V1.2.0 11.05.2022 - updates for SW4.0 V1.3.0 13.07.2022 - updates for SW4.1

Data.Name: alignment_Auto_NVM_filter.K_Max_NVM_Write_driving_cycle
Data.Comment: Calibration value specifies the maximum allowed number of storing into NVM during one driving cycle

Data.Name: alignment_Auto_NVM_filter.K_delta_threshold_value_angle
Data.Comment: Delta threshold angle between stored and current misalignment angle

Data.Name: alignment_Auto_NVM_filter.Min_time_between_write_requests
Data.Comment: This parameter define the minimum time between two succsseive write request from alignment Manager t

Item.Text: AA have not converged in current operation cycle, system are runing on previosly stored data (EOL/ST/OverWritingValue/SA + Stored AA)
AA function has converged (AA value have been stored to NVM)

Data.Name: calibrationNumber
Data.Comment: Calibration Number
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the vehicle manufacturer specific ECU application data identification record.

The format of the application data identification structure shall be ECU specific and defined by the vehicle manufacturer.

Item.Text: Autolignment complete (value can not be written)

Data.Name: cfg.ALIGNMENT_STATUS.CurrentAlignmentAngleUsed
Data.Comment: Azimuth deviation, used for reading the Auto alignment angle

Data.Name: cfg.ALIGNMENT_STATUS.EOLAlignmentAngle
Data.Comment: Azimuth deviation, used for reading the Auto alignment angle

Data.Name: cfg.LSS_TRIGGER.SideRadarFunctionBSWactivation
Data.Comment: BSW activation/deactivation, to activate or deactivate function with all LED warnings, CAN outputs a

Data.Name: cfg.LSS_TRIGGER.SideRadarFunctionELKAactivation
Data.Comment: ELKAactivation/deactivation, to activate or deactivate function with all LED warnings, CAN outputs a

Data.Name: cfg.LSS_TRIGGER.SideRadarFunctionLCWactivation
Data.Comment: LCW activation/deactivation, to activate or deactivate function with all LED warnings, CAN outputs a

Data.Name: cfg.TRACKER_OBJECT_CONF.TrackWidthFront
Data.Comment: Width front axle

Data.Name: cfg.TRACKER_OBJECT_CONF.TrackWidthRear
Data.Comment: Width rear axle

Data.Name: cfg.VEHICLE_CONF.VehicleWidth
Data.Comment: Vehicle width

Data.Name: cfg.VEHICLE_CONF.VehicleLength
Data.Comment: Vehicle length

Data.Name: cfg.VEHICLE_CONF.Architecture_Type
Data.Comment: Architecture Type

Data.Name: cfg.VEHICLE_CONF.Engine_Type
Data.Comment: Thermal or Electric vehicle

Data.Name: cfg.VEHICLE_CONF.Gearbox_Type
Data.Comment: Automatic or manual

Data.Name: cfg.VEHICLE_CONF.SODFL_long
Data.Comment: Position referred to center frontbumper

Data.Name: cfg.VEHICLE_CONF.SODFL_lat
Data.Comment: Position referred to center frontbumper

Data.Name: cfg.VEHICLE_CONF.SODFL_angle_azimuth
Data.Comment: Position referred to center frontbumper, driving direction 0 deg, positive to right side

Data.Name: cfg.VEHICLE_CONF.SODFR_long
Data.Comment: Position referred to center frontbumper

Data.Name: cfg.VEHICLE_CONF.SODFR_lat
Data.Comment: Position referred to center frontbumper

Data.Name: cfg.VEHICLE_CONF.SODFR_angle_azimuth
Data.Comment: Position referred to center frontbumper, driving direction 0 deg, positive to right side

Data.Name: cfg.VEHICLE_CONF.SODRL_long
Data.Comment: Position referred to center frontbumper

Data.Name: cfg.VEHICLE_CONF.SODRL_lat
Data.Comment: Position referred to center frontbumper

Data.Name: cfg.VEHICLE_CONF.SODRL_angle_azimuth
Data.Comment: Position referred to center frontbumper, driving direction 0 deg, positive to right side

Data.Name: cfg.VEHICLE_CONF.SODRR_long
Data.Comment: Position referred to center frontbumper

Data.Name: cfg.VEHICLE_CONF.SODRR_lat
Data.Comment: Position referred to center frontbumper

Data.Name: cfg.VEHICLE_CONF.SODRR_angle_azimuth
Data.Comment: Position referred to center frontbumper, driving direction 0 deg, positive to right side

Data.Name: cfg.VEHICLE_CONF.RearAxleDistance
Data.Comment: From front bumper to rear axle

Data.Name: cfg.VEHICLE_CONF.FrontAxleDistance
Data.Comment: From front bumper to front axle

Data.Name: cfg.VEHICLE_CONF.MPUSelection
Data.Comment: Select MPU (meter or multimedia), choose between ECU which sends activation signals

Data.Name: cfg.VEHICLE_TYPE.Variant_Type
Data.Comment: Variant

Data.Name: cfg.VEHICLE_TYPE.Vehicle_Type
Data.Comment: Type

Item.Text: Alignment condition not ready(Vehicle is moving)
Alignment condition not ready(System failure detected)
Alignment condition not ready(System failure detected and Vehicle moving)

Data.Name: Current_Assignation_pin_value
Data.Comment: Current position value for the radar(see codding)

Data.Name: configurationDataReferenceAfterConfigurationProcess
Data.Comment: Nissan Configuration File
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the vehicle manufacturer spare part number.

Record data content and format shall be server specific and defined by the vehicle manufacturer.

Data.Name: DTCCount
Data.Comment: DTC count

Data.Name: DTCExtendedData.AgingCounter
Data.Comment: Description of the extendedData AgingCounter (use by Failure Page to decode data)

Item.Text: Device and failure type ODB codding
circuit short to ground or open
circuit short to battery or open
signal rate of change below threshold
signal rate of change above threshold
signal bias level out of range / zero adj. failure
signal has too few transitions / events
signal has too many transitions / events
alive / sequence counter incorrect / not updated
value of signal protection calculation incorrect
Component or sys. operation obstructed or blocked

Data.Name: DTCFaultDetectionCounter
Data.Comment: DTC fault detction counter

Data.Name: DTCFormatIdentifier
Data.Comment: DTC format - Renault/Nissan use value 0 (3 bytes DTC)

Data.Name: DTCOccurenceCounter
Data.Comment: Description of the extendedData DTCOccurenceCounter (use by Failure Page to decode data)

Data.Name: ECUSerialNumber
Data.Comment: ECU serial number
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the ECU (server) serial number.

Record data content and format shall be server specific.

Data.Name: EOL_Alignment2.Doopler_lat
Data.Comment: Position referred to center front bumper

Data.Name: EOL_Alignment2.Doopler_long
Data.Comment: Position referred to center front bumper

Data.Name: diagnosticVersion
Data.Comment: VDIAG
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the diagnostic version of the ECU.

It is defined by the vehicle manufacturer.

Refer to manufacture ECU designer.

Data.Name: fingerprint
Data.Comment: Only for programmable ECU
Data.Description:
The value is written by the ECU when executing the SendKey SecurityAccess service

The value can be read in boot loader mode.

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.VEHICLE_CONF.4WS
Data.Comment: 4 Wheels Steering

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.VEHICLE_CONF.VehicleWidth
Data.Comment: Vehicle width

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.VEHICLE_CONF.VehicleLength
Data.Comment: Vehicle length

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.VEHICLE_CONF.Architecture_Type
Data.Comment: Architecture Type

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.VEHICLE_CONF.Engine_Type
Data.Comment: Thermal or Electric vehicle

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.VEHICLE_CONF.Gearbox_Type
Data.Comment: Automatic or manual

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.VEHICLE_CONF.SODFL_long
Data.Comment: Position referred to center frontbumper

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.VEHICLE_CONF.SODFL_lat
Data.Comment: Position referred to center frontbumper

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.VEHICLE_CONF.SODFL_angle_azimuth
Data.Comment: Position referred to center frontbumper, driving direction 0 deg, positive to right side

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.VEHICLE_CONF.SODFR_long
Data.Comment: Position referred to center frontbumper

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.VEHICLE_CONF.SODFR_lat
Data.Comment: Position referred to center frontbumper

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.VEHICLE_CONF.SODFR_angle_azimuth
Data.Comment: Position referred to center frontbumper, driving direction 0 deg, positive to right side

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.VEHICLE_CONF.SODRL_long
Data.Comment: Position referred to center frontbumper

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.VEHICLE_CONF.SODRL_lat
Data.Comment: Position referred to center frontbumper

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.VEHICLE_CONF.SODRL_angle_azimuth
Data.Comment: Position referred to center frontbumper, driving direction 0 deg, positive to right side

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.VEHICLE_CONF.SODRR_long
Data.Comment: Position referred to center frontbumper

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.VEHICLE_CONF.SODRR_lat
Data.Comment: Position referred to center frontbumper

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.VEHICLE_CONF.SODRR_angle_azimuth
Data.Comment: Position referred to center frontbumper, driving direction 0 deg, positive to right side

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.VEHICLE_CONF.RearAxleDistance
Data.Comment: From front bumper to rear axle

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.VEHICLE_CONF.FrontAxleDistance
Data.Comment: From front bumper to front axle

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.VEHICLE_CONF.MPUSelection
Data.Comment: Select MPU (meter or multimedia), choose between ECU which sends activation signals

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.VEHICLE_TYPE.Vehicle_Type
Data.Comment: Vehicle platform

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.VEHICLE_TYPE.Variant_Type
Data.Comment: EE Arhitecture Variant

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.LSS_CONF.BSW_LCWDefault
Data.Comment: BSW/LCW Default mode

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.LSS_CONF.BSW_SWA_inhibition
Data.Comment: Used in BSW-LCW state machine

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.LSS_CONF.BSW_Variable_Zone_Length_SPEED1
Data.Comment: Minimum Host Velocity to define the first Variable zone Length

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.LSS_CONF.BSW_Variable_Zone_Length_SPEED2
Data.Comment: Maximum Host Velocity to define the first Variable zone Length

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.LSS_CONF.BSW_Variable_Zone_Length_SPEED3
Data.Comment: MinimumHost Velocity to define the second Variable zone Length

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.LSS_CONF.BSW_Variable_Zone_Length_SPEED4
Data.Comment: Maximum Host Velocity to define the second Variable zone Length

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.LSS_CONF.BSW_Variable_Zone_Length_SPEED5
Data.Comment: Minimum Host Velocity to define the third Variable zone Length

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.LSS_CONF.BSW_Variable_Zone_Length_SPEED6
Data.Comment: Maximum Host Velocity to define the third Variable zone Length

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.LSS_CONF.BSW_Variable_Zone_Length1
Data.Comment: First Zone length according to the Minimum Host velocity

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.LSS_CONF.BSW_Variable_Zone_Length2
Data.Comment: First Zone length according to the Maximum Host velocity

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.LSS_CONF.BSW_Variable_Zone_Length3
Data.Comment: Second Zone length according to the Minimum Host velocity

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.LSS_CONF.BSW_Variable_Zone_Length4
Data.Comment: Second Zone length according to the Maximum Host velocity

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.LSS_CONF.BSW_Variable_Zone_Length5
Data.Comment: Third Zone length according to the Minimum Host velocity

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.LSS_CONF.BSW_Variable_Zone_Length6
Data.Comment: Third Zone length according to the Maximum Host velocity

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.LSS_CONF.BSW_Variable_Zone_Activation
Data.Comment: Zone Size Change Activation/Deactivation

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.LSS_TRIGGER.SideRadarFunctionBSWactivation
Data.Comment: BSW activation/deactivation, to activate or deactivate function with all LED warnings, CAN outputs and related Err monitorings. Agreement tbd.

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.LSS_TRIGGER.SideRadarFunctionLCWactivation
Data.Comment: LCW activation/deactivation, to activate or deactivate function with all LED warnings, CAN outputs and related Err monitorings. Agreement tbd.

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.LSS_TRIGGER.BSW_Sound_Activation
Data.Comment: Possibility to activiate or deactivate the sound for alert

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.LED.bulb_check_activation
Data.Comment: Request activation or deactivation of the bulb check.

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.LED.bulb_check_duration_time
Data.Comment: Define how long time is the bulb check.

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.LED.bulb_check_luminance
Data.Comment: Define what is the LED luminosity during the bulb check.

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.LED.bulb_check_State
Data.Comment: Bulb check can be solid LED or blinking LED.

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.LED_Luminance.DayBlinkLuminance
Data.Comment: Duty cycle for LED blink at Day(0%-100%)

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.LED_Luminance.NightBlinkLuminance
Data.Comment: Duty cycle for LED blink at Night(0%-100%)

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.LED_Luminance.DayContiniousOnLuminance
Data.Comment: Duty cycle for LED continious on at Day(0%-100%)

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.LED_Luminance.NightContiniousOnLuminance
Data.Comment: Duty cycle for LED continious on at Night(0%-100%)

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.A_BCI_function_availability
Data.Comment: State of CTA function is linked to BCI function

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.OSE_Dev.DelayVisualAlert
Data.Comment: [Pgen_Delay_Visual_Alert]

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.OSE_Dev.ExternalMirrorFrequency
Data.Comment: [Vgen_external_mirror_Frequency]

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.OSE_Dev.CollisionLimitWidth
Data.Comment: [pgen_Collision_Limit_width]

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.OSE_Dev.VehicleState
Data.Comment: [c_vehicle_state]

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.OSE_Dev.InitAlert
Data.Comment: [c_Init_Alert]

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.OSE_Dev.FailureToHMI
Data.Comment: [c_failure_to_HMI]

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.OSE_Dev.InitForActivation
Data.Comment: [pgen_Init_For_Activation]

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.OSE_Dev.OSE_MinLongiTargetSpeed
Data.Comment: [OSE_Min_Longi_Target_Speed]

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.OSE_Dev.TTC_AlertLevel1Threshold
Data.Comment: [pgen_TTC_Alert_Level1_Threshold]

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.OSE_Dev.TTC_AlertLevel2Threshold
Data.Comment: [pgen_TTC_Alert_Level2_Threshold]

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.OSE_Dev.Alert_Level2
Data.Comment: [c_Alert_Level2]

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.OSE_Dev.OSE_DeactivationFailure
Data.Comment: [c_OSE_Deactivation_for_Failure]

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.OSE_Dev.CollisionLimitPosition
Data.Comment: [Pgen_Collision_Limit_Position]

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.OSE_Dev.ResetState
Data.Comment: [c_OSE_ResetState]

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.OSE_Dev.Hysteresis
Data.Comment: [c_OSE_Hysteresis]

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.OSE_Dev.Hysteresis_Min
Data.Comment: [c_OSE_Hysteresis_Min]

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.OSE_Dev.Hysteresis_Max
Data.Comment: [c_OSE_Hysteresis_Max]

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.OSE_TRIGGER.SideRadarFunctionOSEactivation
Data.Comment: [c_OSE_Activation_Switch]

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.RCTA_DEV.RCTA_TTC_NOM
Data.Comment: Target time to collision from ego. When value is out of the range, the value shall be automatically adapted to the limit.

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.RCTA_DEV.DETECTION_ZONE_REAR
Data.Comment: - Distance zone from bumper to rear to define the CTA zone limit. - value send via DID will be unsigned and the "-" will be added in code. - When value is out of the range, the value shall be automatically adapted to the limit.

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.RCTA_DEV.DETECTION_ZONE_SIDE
Data.Comment: - Distance zone from bumper to front to define the CTA zone limit. - When value is out of the range, the value shall be automatically adapted to the limit.

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.RCTA_DEV.RCTA_EGO_SPEED_MAX_deactivation
Data.Comment: - Speed max vehicle fitted with side radars to have RCTA activated - When value is out of the range

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.RCTA_DEV.RCTA_EGO_SPEED_MIN_deactivation
Data.Comment: - Speed min vehicle fitted with side radars to have RCTA activated - value send via DID will be uns

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.RCTA_DEV.RCTA_TARGET_SPEED_MIN
Data.Comment: - Target speed min to get an alert - When value is out of the range, the value shall be automatical

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.RCTA_DEV.RCTA_TARGET_SPEED_MAX
Data.Comment: - Target speed max to get an alert - When value is out of the range, the value shall be automatical

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.RCTA_DEV.RCTA_ANGLE_MIN
Data.Comment: - Approching angle min to consider the target as relevant. - When value is out of the range, the value shall be automatically adapted to the limit.

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.RCTA_DEV.RCTA_ANGLE_MAX
Data.Comment: - Approching angle max to consider the target as relevant. - When value is out of the range, the value shall be automatically adapted to the limit.

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.RCTA_DEV.TempoWhenRearGearTransitions
Data.Comment: Filter rear gear concidered as selected, for manual and automatic gear box

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.RCTA_DEV.RCTASoundActivationAlert1
Data.Comment: Possibility to deactivate the sound for alert level 1.

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.RCTA_DEV.ResetRCTAState
Data.Comment: RCTA state after a reset (reset request done by customer on HMI). This DIDs specifies the default state for RCTA after a "RCTA State Reset".

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.RCTA_DEV.RCTASoundActivationAlert2
Data.Comment: Possibility to deactivate the sound for alert level 2.

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.RCTA_DEV.RCTAledManagement
Data.Comment: Activate one or two LEDs for an alert.

Item.Text: LED is switched ON for the side related to the alert
LED left AND right are switched ON independently of where is coming the alert

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.RCTA_DEV.RCTAledSolidBlinking
Data.Comment: Mirror LED : possility to have a solid LED alert or blinking LED alert

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.RCTA_DEV.RCTA_AutoReactivation
Data.Comment: RCTA activation state (prm.RCTA_SystemState) after new ignition

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.RCTA_DEV.RCTAdelayLED_ON
Data.Comment: When an alert is ON, the LED managed by the side radar shall be deferrable When an alert is ON, LED is switch on with delay (be synchronized with the IVI delay due to processing time) When an alert is OFF, LED is switch OFF with delay (be synchronized with the IVI delay due to processing time)

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.RCTA_TRIGGER.SideRadarFunctionRCTAactivation
Data.Comment: RCTA activation/deactivation, to activate or deactivate function with all LED warnings, CAN outputs and related Error monitorings (see RCTA statemachine) This info shall have highest priority for deactivation and shall deactivate RCTA LED and RCTA failsafe.

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.BSIS_DEV.INFORMATON_ZONE_LAT1
Data.Comment: Lateral distance between vehicle side and the neareast side of information zone.

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.BSIS_DEV.INFORMATON_ZONE_LAT2
Data.Comment: Lateral distance between vehicle side and the furthest side of information zone.

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.BSIS_DEV.BSIS_UC2_EXIT_Dist
Data.Comment: Distance that the target will travel before stopping the warning in the dynamic UC2

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.BSIS_DEV.BSIS_EGO_SPEED_MAX
Data.Comment: Speed max vehicle fitted with side radars to have BSIS activated

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.BSIS_DEV.BSIS_EGO_SPEED_HYST
Data.Comment: Parameter used for the hysteresis of the EGO speed max in dynamic use cases to avoid alert toggling

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.BSIS_DEV.BSIS_STAT_EGO_SPEED_MAX
Data.Comment: Target speed max to get an alert in a static use case

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.BSIS_DEV.BSIS_TARGET_SPEED_MIN
Data.Comment: Target speed min to get an alert

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.BSIS_DEV.BSIS_TARGET_SPEED_MAX
Data.Comment: Target speed max to get an alert

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.BSIS_DEV.TempoWhenGearTransitions
Data.Comment: Filter rear gear concidered as selected, for manual and automatic gear box (The system shall wait a delay defined by parameter TempoWhenRearGearTransitions (default value : 0.2s) before taking in account gear position.)

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.BSIS_TRIGGER.SideRadarFunctionBSISactivation
Data.Comment: BSIS activation/deactivation, to activate or deactivate function with all warnings, CAN outputs and related Error monitorings (see BSIS statemachine) This info shall have highest priority for deactivation.

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.BSIS_Driver_Position
Data.Comment: left or right

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.SWAS_Present
Data.Comment: SWAS ECU presence in the vehicle

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.BSIS_Deactivation_By_User
Data.Comment: Possibility t deactivate/activate BSIS by the user

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.BSIS_Radar_On_Open_Transports
Data.Comment: Mounting position of the Rear sensors

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.BSIS_AutoReactivation
Data.Comment: BSIS activation state after new ignition

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.ConfigurationDataReferenceAfterConfigurationProcess
Data.Comment: Configuration Data Reference

Data.Name: Mileage
Data.Comment: Description of the extendedData Mileage (use by Failure Page to decode data)

Data.Name: prm.ALIGNMENT_STATUS.angle_Initial_NVM_Azimuth
Data.Comment: Initial Azimuth Alignment (SA,EOL,OvverwriteDID) angle value stored into NVM

Data.Name: prm.ALIGNMENT_STATUS.angle_Initial_NVM_Vertical
Data.Comment: Initial Vertical Alignment (SA,EOL,OvverwriteDID) angle value stored into NVM

Item.Text: Stationnary Alignment angle Out of range / Target Outside Tolerance
Target Outside Tolerance / Stationnary Alignment angle Out of range
Service Alignment angle Out of rang
No Inital alignment has been done
Overwrite (Inital alignment has been done via DID write operation)
Last attempt to perform Inital calibration was not sucessfull (To be retained in NVM over operation cycles)
Reserved (Initial alignment reset to default state due to Internal failure)
No Inital alignment has been done
Overwrite (Inital alignment has been done via DID write operation)
Last attempt to perform Inital calibration was not sucessfull (To be retained in NVM over operation cycles)
Reserved (Initial alignment reset to default state due to Internal failure)

Data.Name: prm.LSS_STATUS.BSW_Alert
Data.Comment: DID for CAN State Machine (BSW-LCW)

Data.Name: prm.LSS_STATUS.LCW_Alert
Data.Comment: DID for CAN State Machine (BSW-LCW)

Data.Name: prm.LSS_STATUS.StateMachine
Data.Comment: DID for CAN State Machine (BSW-LCW)

Data.Name: ptng.LSS_CONF.BSW_LCW_Default
Data.Comment: BSW/LCW Default mode

Data.Name: ptng.LSS_CONF.BSW_SWA_inhibition
Data.Comment: Used in BSW-LCW state machine

Data.Name: ptng.LSS_CONF.BSW_Variable_Zone_Activation
Data.Comment: Zone Size Change Activation/Deactivation

Data.Name: ptng.LSS_CONF.BSW_Variable_Zone_Length_SPEED1
Data.Comment: Minimum Host Velocity to define the first Variable zone Length

Data.Name: ptng.LSS_CONF.BSW_Variable_Zone_Length_SPEED2
Data.Comment: Maximum Host Velocity to define the first Variable zone Length

Data.Name: ptng.LSS_CONF.BSW_Variable_Zone_Length_SPEED3
Data.Comment: Minimum Host Velocity to define the second Variable zone Length

Data.Name: ptng.LSS_CONF.BSW_Variable_Zone_Length_SPEED4
Data.Comment: Maximum Host Velocity to define the second Variable zone Length

Data.Name: ptng.LSS_CONF.BSW_Variable_Zone_Length_SPEED5
Data.Comment: Minimum Host Velocity to define the third Variable zone Length

Data.Name: ptng.LSS_CONF.BSW_Variable_Zone_Length1
Data.Comment: First Zone length according to the Minimum Host velocity

Data.Name: ptng.LSS_CONF.BSW_Variable_Zone_Length2
Data.Comment: First Zone length according to the Maximum Host velocity

Data.Name: ptng.LSS_CONF.BSW_Variable_Zone_Length3
Data.Comment: Second Zone length according to the Minimum Host velocity

Data.Name: ptng.LSS_CONF.BSW_Variable_Zone_Length4
Data.Comment: Second Zone length according to the Maximum Host velocity

Data.Name: ptng.LSS_CONF.BSW_Variable_Zone_Length5
Data.Comment: Third Zone length according to the Minimum Host velocity

Data.Name: ptng.LSS_CONF.BSW_Variable_Zone_Length6
Data.Comment: Third Zone length according to the Maximum Host velocity

Data.Name: ptng.LSS_CONF.BSW_Variable_Zone_Length_SPEED6
Data.Comment: Maximum Host Velocity to define the third Variable zone Length

Data.Name: ptng.RCTA_DEV.DETECTION_ZONE_REAR
Data.Comment: DID = fXMinBreakthrough

Data.Name: ptng.RCTA_DEV.DETECTION_ZONE_SIDE
Data.Comment: DID = fXMaxBreakthrough

Data.Name: ptng.RCTA_DEV.RCTA_ANGLE_MAX
Data.Comment: fMaxHeadingAngle

Data.Name: ptng.RCTA_DEV.RCTA_ANGLE_MIN
Data.Comment: fMinHeadingAngl

Data.Name: ptng.RCTA_DEV.RCTA_AutoReactivation
Data.Comment: RCTA activation state (prm.RCTA_SystemState) after new ignition

Data.Name: ptng.RCTA_DEV.RCTA_EGO_SPEED_MAX_deactivation
Data.Comment: DID = fVEgoMax

Data.Name: ptng.RCTA_DEV.RCTA_EGO_SPEED_MIN_deactivation
Data.Comment: DID = fVEgoMin

Data.Name: ptng.RCTA_DEV.RCTA_TARGET_SPEED_MAX
Data.Comment: Target speed max to get an alert

Data.Name: ptng.RCTA_DEV.RCTA_TARGET_SPEED_MIN
Data.Comment: Target speed min to get an alert

Data.Name: ptng.RCTA_DEV.RCTA_TTC_NOM
Data.Comment: Target time to collision from ego

Data.Name: ptng.RCTA_DEV.RCTAdelayLED_ON
Data.Comment: When an alert is ON, the LED managed by the side radar shall be deferrable

Data.Name: ptng.RCTA_DEV.RCTAledManagement
Data.Comment: Activate one or two LEDs for an alert. Feedback to be provided by Conti if this activation is possib

Data.Name: ptng.RCTA_DEV.RCTASoundActivationAlert1
Data.Comment: Possibility to deactivate the sound for alert level 1.

Data.Name: ptng.RCTA_DEV.RCTASoundActivationAlert2
Data.Comment: Possibility to deactivate the sound for alert level 2.

Data.Name: ptng.RCTA_DEV.RCTAledSolidBlinking
Data.Comment: Mirror LED : possility to have a solid LED alert or blinking LED alert

Data.Name: ptng.RCTA_DEV.ResetRCTAState
Data.Comment: RCTA state after a reset (reset request done by customer on HMI). This DIDs specifies the default st

Data.Name: ptng.RCTA_DEV.TempoWhenRearGearTransitions
Data.Comment: Filter rear gear concidered as selected, for manual and automatic gear box

Application instruction Resets (e.g. Stack overflow, Wdg,)
Application instruction Resets (e.g. Stack overflow, Wdg,)
Application instruction Resets (e.g. Stack overflow, Wdg,)
Application instruction Resets (e.g. Stack overflow, Wdg,)

Data.Comment: Routine information

Data.Comment: Routine information

Data.Comment: Routine information

Data.Comment: Routine information

Data.Comment: Routine information

Item.Text: Stationary Alignment angle Out of range

Data.Name: RoutineStatusRecord.FactoryMisalignementAngle
Data.Comment: Routine information

Data.Comment: Routine information

Data.Name: RoutineStatusRecord.FactoryRoutineInfo

Data.Comment: Routine information

Item.Text: Stationnary Alignment angle Out of range / Target Outside Tolerance
Target Outside Tolerance / Stationnary Alignment angle Out of range
Service Alignment angle Out of rang

Data.Name: SessionName
Data.Comment: Diagnostic session values. See documentation for more information.
Data.Description:
[0x00] : ISO / SAE Reserved
[0x01] : Default session
[0x02] : Programming session
[0x03] : Extended diagnostic session
[0x04] : Safety system diagnostic session
[0x05 - 0x3F] : ISO / SAE Reserved
[0x40 - 0x5F] : Vehicle manufacturer specific
[0x60 - 0x7E] : System supplier specific
[0x7F] : ISO / SAE Reserved

Data.Name: snpsht.BatteryVoltage.CAN_BatteryVoltage
Data.Comment: Battery voltage on CAN

Data.Name: snpsht.BatteryVoltage.Internal_BatteryVoltage
Data.Comment: Internal Battery Voltage

Data.Name: SystemNameOrEngineType
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the system supplier specific ECU (server) software version number.

Record data content and format shall be server specific and defined by the system supplier.

Data.Name: systemSupplierECUSoftwareNumber
Data.Comment: Software Number (Supplier)
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the system supplier specific ECU (server) software number.

Record data content and format shall be server specific and defined by the system supplier.

Data.Name: systemSupplierECUSoftwareVersionNumber
Data.Comment: Software Version (Supplier)
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the system supplier specific ECU (server) software version number.

Record data content and format shall be server specific and defined by the system supplier.

Data.Name: systemSupplierIdentifier
Data.Comment: Name and address information of the supplier.
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the system supplier name and address information.

Record data content and format shall be server specific and defined by the system supplier.

Data.Name: vehicleManufacturerECUHardwareNumber
Data.Comment: Hardware Number
Data.Description:
This value shall be used by reading services to reference the vehicle-manufacturer-specific ECU (server) hardware number.

Record data content and format shall be serverspecific and defined by vehicle manufacturer.

Data.Name: VehicleManufacturerKitAssemblyPartNumber
Data.Comment: Calibration Number
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the vehicle manufacturer specific ECU application data identification record.

The format of the application data identification structure shall be ECU specific and defined by the vehicle manufacturer.

Data.Name: vehicleManufacturerSparePartNumber
Data.Comment: Ordered Part Number - This value shall contain the number of the part ordered by OEM
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the vehicle manufacturer spare part number.

Record data content and format shall be server specific and defined by the vehicle manufacturer.

Data.Name: VIN
Data.Comment: Vehicle Identification Number
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the VIN number.

Record data content and format shall be specified by the vehicle manufacturer.

It shall not be erased when the ECU is reprogrammed.

Data.Name: ptng.RCTA_DEV.RCTA_EGO_SPEED_MAX_reactivation
Data.Comment: When the ego speed exceed the value RCTA_EGO_SPEED_MAX, the CTA alert is OFF. To have CTA alert comi

Data.Name: ptng.RCTA_DEV.RCTA_EGO_SPEED_MIN_reactivation
Data.Comment: When the ego speed exceed the value RCTA_EGO_SPEED_MIN, the CTA alert is OFF. To have CTA alert comi

Data.Name: ptng.BSIS_DEV.ALERT_HOLD_DURATION
Data.Comment: The time duration that the alert will be active once the target leaves the BSIS information zone.

Data.Name: vehicleManufacturerSparePartNumber_N
Data.Comment: Nissan part number
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the vehicle manufacturer spare part number.

Record data content and format shall be server specific and defined by the vehicle manufacturer.

Data.Name: Plant_Alignment_Recorder_DID.Date_Year
Data.Comment: Year

Data.Name: Aftersales_Alignment_recorder_DID.Date_Year_0
Data.Comment: Year

Data.Name: Aftersales_Alignment_recorder_DID.Date_Year_1
Data.Comment: Year

Data.Name: Aftersales_Alignment_recorder_DID.Date_Year_2
Data.Comment: Year

Data.Name: Aftersales_Alignment_recorder_DID.Date_Year_3
Data.Comment: Year

Data.Name: Aftersales_Alignment_recorder_DID.Date_Year_4
Data.Comment: Year

Data.Name: Aftersales_Alignment_recorder_DID.Date_Year_5
Data.Comment: Year

Data.Name: Aftersales_Alignment_recorder_DID.Date_Year_6
Data.Comment: Year

Data.Name: Aftersales_Alignment_recorder_DID.Date_Year_7
Data.Comment: Year

Data.Name: Aftersales_Alignment_recorder_DID.Date_Year_8
Data.Comment: Year

Data.Name: Aftersales_Alignment_recorder_DID.Date_Year_9
Data.Comment: Year

Data.Name: OutputPermanentControlList
Data.Comment: List of Data Identifiers for Input Output Control - The list defined here is AN EXAMPLE.
Data.Description:
Definition of IOControl

Data: IOCTL.DataIdentifierList

  Contains the list of all IO devices that may be
  controlled by InputOutputControl service.
 
  Example:
 
    0 - DID_Pump
    1 - DID_Wiper

If some of these IO devices have parameters, you should defined a data with the same name as the IO devices followed by a dot and a text

  Example:
 
  Data: DID_Wiper.Mode
     Type: List
       0 - Slow speed
       1 - High speed

Then, you should add theses data in the "Send tab" of the InputOutpuControl request, after the IOCTL.OptionRecord.Parameter data.

Data.Name: OutputControlCommand
Data.Comment: Input Output control parameter list

Data.Name: RoutineControlType
Data.Comment: Routine control type - VALUES OTHER THAN 1, 2 & 3 ARE RESERVED AND SHOULD NOT BE USED.

Data.Name: RoutineIdentifierList
Data.Comment: Routine identifier - This is an example. See documentation for more information.
Data.Description:
Definition of RoutineControl

Data: RoutineIdentifierList

  Contains the list of all routine identifier that may be used with InputOutputControl service.
 
  Example:
  
    0x4501 - ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave

If some of these routine identifer have parameters, you should defined a data with the same name as the routine identifer followed by a dot and a text

  Example:
 
  Data: ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave.Min
  Data: ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave.Max
  Data: ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave.Freq

Then, you should add theses data in the "Send tab" of the RoutineControl request, after the RoutineIdentifierList data.

Data.Name: RoutineStatusRecord.RoutineInfo
Data.Comment: Routine information

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.RCTA_DEV.RCTA_EGO_SPEED_MAX_reactivation
Data.Comment: When the ego speed exceed the value [RCTA_EGO_SPEED_MAX_deactivation], the CTA alert is OFF. To have

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.RCTA_DEV.RCTA_EGO_SPEED_MIN_reactivation
Data.Comment: - When the ego speed exceed the value [RCTA_EGO_SPEED_MIN_deactivation], the CTA alert is OFF. To ha

Data.Name: prm.LSS_STATUS.BSWLCW_SystemState
Data.Comment: DID for CAN State Machine (BSW-LCW)

Data.Name: cfg.VEHICLE_CONF.4WS
Data.Comment: Four Wheel Steering

Data.Name: cfg.RRRL_Radar_Config.BSIS_DEV_ALERT_HOLD_DURATION
Data.Comment: The time duration that the alert will be active once the target leaves the BSIS information zone.

Data.Name: cfg.LSS_TRIGGER.BSW_AutoReactivation
Data.Comment: Parameter for AutoReactivation of BSW feature

Item.Text: No possible classification about the type of fault
Open circuit or Short circuit to ground
Open circuit or Short circuit to battery

Data.Name: RoutineIdentifier
Data.Comment: Routine Identifier

Request.Name: StartDiagnosticSession.Default
Request.Comment: Standard definition for DDT2000 automatic screen

Request.Name: StartDiagnosticSession.Programming
Request.Comment: Standard definition for DDT2000 automatic screen

Request.Name: StartDiagnosticSession
Request.Comment: Standard definition for DDT2000 automatic screen

Request.Name: StartDiagnosticSession.ExtendedDiagnostic
Request.Comment: Standard definition for DDT2000 automatic screen

Request.Name: ClearDiagnosticInformation.All
Request.Comment: The ClearDiagnosticInformation service is used by the tool to clear diagnostic information in an ECUs memory

Request.Name: ReadDTCInformation.ReportDTC
Request.Comment: DDT2000 Failures page requests / DDT : Unusable for Clip import

Request.Name: ReadDTCInformation.ReportSnapshot
Request.Comment: DDT2000 Failures page requests / Clip import : Unusable in the standard tools of DDT2000

Request.Name: ReadDTCInformation.ReportSupportedDTC_CPDD Import
Request.Comment: Report supported DTC for Clip import

Request.Name: ReportDTCInformation.ReportDTCFaultDetectionCounter_CPDD Import
Request.Comment: Report DTC fault detection counter counter for Clip import

Request.Name: TesterPresent.WithResponse
Request.Comment: This request enable P2P tester present for tool (Screen, IOctl, ...)

Label.Text: Condition to turn on LED : IGN ON + Vehicle Speed = 0kph
